certificate.description: Highly integrated, low power, security processor with rich peripheral connection options like USB, NFC, Contacted Smart Card, SPI, UART, ADC, etc.

rows: SBL Version: 1.1 SBI Version: 1.0 AAI Version: 2.1
The module only supports an Approved mode of operation.

Clock - 26MHz clock - 32KHz clock; Clock output - 26MHz clock output
One reset input; Reset output: Indicates that system power supply is stable.
Code and data from SPI flash (clock, device select, and four data I/O). All Code/Data Input is authenticated by the module.
Data Input Data Output Control Input Status Output
Service request input; Service response output; (USB differential data bus); Device interface used by the CO to make service requests. Requests are authenticated via the KAS secure session.
Status output (Four UART ports of four signals each.)

Over 50 power and ground pins. Power is distributed to the chip using designated IO and core power pins that are completely separated from any signal pin groups. Power pins are only connected to the internal power planes of the silicon chip.
The CO role is authenticated by the verification of an ECDSA digital signature using a P-256 key.
The probability that a random attempt will succeed or a false acceptance will occur is 1/2^128 which is less than 1/1,000,000.

Based on performance limitations, the probability of successfully authenticating to the module within one minute is 3,750/2^128 which is less than 1/100,000. The module will only allow one attempt to verify the CO – if that attempt fails, the module will be in an error state and must be rebooted to try and become operational again
